title: refactor: make TTS provider dispatch explicit
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
- Renames `generateSpeech` to the provider-specific `generateElevenLabsSpeech`.
- Keeps ElevenLabs and Qwen upstream model-ID lookup inside their adapters.
- Replaces implicit TTS fallthrough and type guards with explicit provider dispatch.
- Adds focused coverage for all three ElevenLabs TTS mappings.

Follow-up to #12525.

Tests: `npm run typecheck`; 10 ElevenLabs audio tests; Qwen routing integration test.

gist.headline: The voices found their lanes
gist.blurb: The audio hive now sends each voice provider down its own clearly marked garden path. ElevenLabs mappings got a small fence of tests, because mysterious fallthrough is not a personality trait.
gist.summary: Refactored gen.pollinations.ai TTS routing to explicitly dispatch ElevenLabs and Qwen providers, renamed generateSpeech to generateElevenLabsSpeech, and added coverage for all three ElevenLabs model mappings.
gist.impact: Audio routing is now easier to maintain and verify, with provider-specific model lookup remaining inside each adapter and focused tests guarding the ElevenLabs mappings.
gist.visual_concept: Polly acts as a cozy garden dispatcher beside three clearly labeled voice-pipeline paths, guiding little sound-wave parcels toward an ElevenLabs adapter station while a retro terminal confirms the correct routes. Code-plants and small test-check flowers represent explicit routing and mapping coverage.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly, the cute yellow-and-brown striped bee mascot with pink blush, light blue wings, and a tiny dispatcher cap, happily directing glowing sound-wave parcels along clearly labeled provider paths beside a retro lime green (#ecf874) terminal and blooming code-plants with checkmark flowers. Cozy 8-bit pixel art aesthetic, soft pastel gradients, warm lighting, retro gaming vibes like Stardew Valley or A Short Hike, with subtle pixel-font text: "TTS ROUTES".
